title: Authenticated Encryption with AES-GCM · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Aead Contract
summary: A multi-tenant service reuses GCM nonces after restart, omits tenant and version context from additional data, and releases plaintext before authentication succeeds. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the aead contract increment in versioned AES-GCM record envelope. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.
Build a bounded decrypt-only adapter that authenticates format, suppresses oracles, records migration, and never emits new legacy ciphertext.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Aead Contract · Theory Crypto Go Aead Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 7789 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example cryptographic engineering team handles case 7789 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication. A defensible solution applies Crypto Go Aead Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned AES-GCM record envelope.

content.code:
package main

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	key := make([]byte, 16)
	key[0] = 2
	block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
	if err != nil { panic(err) }
	aead, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
	if err != nil { panic(err) }
	nonce := make([]byte, aead.NonceSize())
	nonce[len(nonce)-1] = 2
	aad := []byte("v1|tenant-a|record-2")
	ciphertext := aead.Seal(nil, nonce, []byte("private-evidence"), aad)
	plaintext, openErr := aead.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, aad)
	tampered := append([]byte(nil), ciphertext...)
	tampered[len(tampered)-1] ^= 1
	_, tamperErr := aead.Open(nil, nonce, tampered, aad)
	fmt.Printf("opened=%t plaintext=%s tamper_rejected=%t\n", openErr == nil, plaintext, tamperErr != nil)
}
